## Already generated by Rust — do NOT recreate
- `{{absSubprojectPath}}/.claude/commands/stack.md` — manifests, dependencies, source extensions, clusters. **Do NOT write or edit stack.md.**
- `{{absSubprojectPath}}/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` (+ `references/examples.md`) — one per qualified cluster, `appliesTo` populated. **Do NOT create any SKILL.md.** A `_no-patterns.md` marker means no cluster qualified — leave it as is.
- `{{absSubprojectPath}}/.claude/commands/guards.md` — a deterministic SEED (existing CLAUDE.md guards + architecture-derived boundary rules). You EXTEND it; you never replace it.
- entity-registry clusters — surfaced in the blocks above; trust them, do not re-derive.

## Your only job (the prose the deterministic pass cannot infer)

1. **Enrich `guards.md`.** Read `{{absSubprojectPath}}/.claude/commands/guards.md` FIRST (it exists). Then read 3-5 representative files — prefer the verified paths in the "Verified samples" block above, so you never re-walk the tree. Append project-specific DO/DON'T rules you can back with a real file you read this turn. Preserve every seeded rule; only add. Rules only — no fenced code blocks.
2. **(Optional) Improve skill descriptions.** For an existing generated `SKILL.md` whose `description:` reads thin, you MAY `Edit` ONLY that frontmatter line into a pushier "Use when {trigger}, {trigger}. Even if the user just says '{casual phrase}'." trigger. Never touch the body, `appliesTo`, or `name`.
3. **Update CLAUDE.md `## Guards` prose.** Read `{{absSubprojectPath}}/CLAUDE.md` first, then Edit ONLY the `## Guards` section to summarise the top rules. Leave `## Stack` / `## Commands` / `## Scan References` / `## Recommended Skills` to Rust.

## Execution rules
- **Never ask the user for confirmation** — `/scan --enrich` is the approval. Proceed autonomously.
- **Read before Write/Edit** — Claude Code rejects edits to a file not read in this context.
- **English only.** Every generated/edited artefact keeps the `<!-- mustard:generated at:{ISO} role:{{role}} -->` header.
- **EVIDENCE RULE:** every DO/DON'T or claim you write MUST trace to a specific file you actually read this turn (cite `path:line`). No speculation, no generic best-practices — if you did not read it here, do not write it.
